title: podman system service API does not honor cgroup settings in containers.conf
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm building a containerized network simulation and development environment and I'm running podman within podman (as documented here: https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-container). I am able to launch containers directly using the podman cli. However, if I try to launch the same configuration via the `podman system service` API I get conmon/cgroup sandboxing errors. It appears that the API process is not honoring some (or maybe all) `containers.conf` settings.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Launch podman within podman like this: `podman run --rm --name podman-test --privileged quay.io/podman/stable podman system service --time=0 unix:///root/podman.sock`. Exec into the `podman-test` container in a different terminal.

2. In the `podman-test` container, pull down the alpine image: `podman pull alpine`. Then create a container via the API like this: `curl --unix-socket /root/podman.sock -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"Image": "alpine", "Cmd": ["echo", "hello"]}' -X POST http://localhost/containers/create`

3. Attempt to start the container using the CID from above: `curl --unix-socket /root/podman.sock -X POST http://localhost/containers/${CID}/start`. 

**Describe the results you received:**

The creation of the container finishes and returns a container ID. However, the start command causes an error. The error from the service API is this:

```
time="2021-10-21T17:39:55Z" level=warning msg="Failed to add conmon to cgroupfs sandbox cgroup: error creating cgroup path /libpod_parent/conmon: write /sys/fs/cgroup/cgroup.subtree_control: device or resource busy"
@ - - [21/Oct/2021:17:39:54 +0000] "POST /containers/256eb18c6270489820b6c3fd1599db46a4f4629b6dca426a35697a8ec9980841/start HTTP/1.1" 500 230 "" "curl/7.76.1"
```

And the error returned to the curl command attempting to start the containers is this:

```
{"cause":"OCI runtime error","message":"writing file `/sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-256eb18c6270489820b6c3fd1599db46a4f4629b6dca426a35697a8ec9980841/cgroup.procs`: Operation not supported: OCI runtime error","response":500}
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

The container should start successfully like it does with direct cli usage.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

It doesn't matter whether the parent podman container is rootfull or rootless.

Using the podman cli directly works fine:

```
podman create alpine echo hello
CID=...
podman start -a ${CID}
```

In addition, if I use `podman create` but then try and start it via the API (curl to `.../start`) then that works fine. So it's the creation/definition of the container via the API that is problematic.

I have also tried copying the `/etc/containers/containers.conf` file to `/usr/share/containers/containers.conf` before staging the API service in case it is ignoring the `/etc/` location but the error still happens.

I have tried all the above with an custom built Ubuntu equivalent of the `quay.io/podman/stable` and get the same results.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.0
API Version:  3.4.0
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Thu Sep 30 19:40:21 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  arch: amd64

  os: linux

  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: false

    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json

  slirp4netns:

      libslirp: 4.4.0

      libseccomp: 2.5.0
  swapFree: 11999899648

  uptime: 1h 54m 44.45s (Approximately 0.04 days)

      Version: |-

        FUSE library version 3.10.4
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,fsync=0
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage

    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 0
  runRoot: /run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 3.4.0
  Built: 1633030821
  BuiltTime: Thu Sep 30 19:40:21 2021

  Version: 3.4.0
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.4.0-1.fc34.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

The host system is Ubuntu 20.04, kernel 5.11.0-38-generic, systemd 245.4-4ubuntu3.13), with cgroup v2 enabled via `systemd.unified_cgroup_hierarchy=1` kernel parameter (validated that `/sys/fs/cgroup/cgroup.controllers` exists).

comments.body:
Here is what I get if I enabled `--log-level debug` for the system service API.

The logs from startup (it does seem to be reading `containers.conf`):

```

And here are the logs from the start request:

```
time="2021-10-21T18:12:57Z" level=debug msg="IdleTracker:new 0m+0h/2t connection(s)" X-Reference-Id=0xc000511540

I think we only expect `containers.conf` to be honored for the Libpod endpoints - for the Docker-compat ones we will continue to use the same defaults to ensure compatibility. @rhatdan @baude Am I correct here?

I understand the compatibility desire but shouldn't there be a way to override critical settings (like podman cgroup behavior) that aren't exposed in the HTTP API (e.g. with the CONTAINERS_CONF env variable or with a specific command line option). Otherwise there are cases where docker API language bindings cannot be used with podman even though direct podman calls work fine (e.g. my situation).

You mention libpod endpoints. Is there a way to start a libpod API service that is provides a docker-style restful API? I'm not finding documentation for that.
I believe containers.conf should override the defaults even in legacy mode, unless it breaks the API.
The Libpod API is accessed via the same REST socket as our Docker-compat API, with documentation here: https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think this has been answered, reopen if I am mistaken.

Hello,

I use `Jetbrains IntelliJ` to connect to the `Docker` api. I don't have control on the way IntelliJ communicates with `Docker`.
All I can do is point `IntelliJ` to `podman` unix socket. 
Everything works fine except I need to pass the following configuration to all containers started by IntelliJ:
```
[containers]
no_hosts=true
```
Pushing that into `~/.config/containers/containers.conf` has no impact.

The issue was moved from `volunteers-wanted` to `I think this has been answered`. Not sure what is the final take on this.
